description: A14FJ - Day 4, part 3: Checking the LM EPS

tokens: These are busy times for the crew of Apollo 14, performing numerous tasks that require to be cleared before their final approach to the ever-closing Moon.
A previously unscheduled battery test on the Lunar Module will make this period somewhat more hectic than anticipated.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Editor's note:All transcript times are presented according to the GET update at 054:53:36 that saw the mission timer moved forward 40 minutes, 2.90 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: We're starting to remove the tunnel hardware.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And are both Al and Ed on the headsets now?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Looking at the procedure here for the business in theLM, looks like the better way to handle it, rather than have you write it all down, is for me to just give it to you on a step-by-step basis after you're in theLMand set up and ready to go.
And I'll just have somebody, Joe here, checking me off as we do it.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: We'll let you know when we're in there, and we'll keep Command Module comm in there.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Just a quick comment in passing.
The Moon is out my rendezvous window right now, and it seems to be growing noticeably in size.
We've reached that point where the - we're running downhill very rapidly toward it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Showing you about - oh, it looks like about 205,000 out, now.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Hey, Fred, old boy, this Moon-view attitude is just what it says.
It's got her framed right in the hatch window.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Yes, that's just like everything else.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: This is Apollo Control at 77 hours 55 minutesGETtime.

There will not be a change of shift briefing with the Maroon team flight director, Milton Windler, in as much as he plans to stay in the control center during this checkout of the Lunar Module battery system.
The next change of shift press briefing will be following the end of the Gold team shift.
This is Apollo Control still live on air-to-ground in the mission of Apollo 14.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: I'm through the tunnel and ingressing theLMat this point.
What's about the first thing you want me to head for?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, I'll let Joe talk to you here and kind of give you a few words on the big picture.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: What this is going to amount to is an abbreviated - a very abbreviated powerup, and we will transfer toLMpower; so, Stu, you can stand by, and we'll get our own times on that.
Now, Ed, if we have a comm problem any time during this - and we are out of configuration - just get the activation checklist and go to page 1-17 and complete the deactivation from that point on.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And, Ed, if you're ready to go now, we'll start through here.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: How about giving me a quick overall picture of the problem as you saw it from the ground.
I didn't see much of anything here that was indicative of a problem.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I wanted to get the values correct here.TMpicked up about 3/10ths of a voltage - of a volt low on battery 5, which gave some concern, and, primarily, we just want to verify that perhaps we're not - we don't have a gauge that's giving us that much of a difference, rather than the battery itself.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Mission Control really does run a tight ship.

Being 0.3 volts short of 37 volts means only a very small loss in capacity for a battery that is more than big enough to do the job, and that has more than enough backup in case it should fail prematurely.
However, 37 volts was the expected value and 36.7 volts is out of tolerance.
They treat all problems in vital systems as potential safety issues until they can convince themselves that it is indeed only a minor problem and not an early indication of something more serious.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: As I recall, we read - Roger.
As I recall, we read them off a half a volt different, 36 and a half to 37.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: That was half a volt onboard, and the telemetry showed the 3/10ths down here.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And essentially, Ed, what we're going to do is put the - put both batteries on an open bus to read out both bus and battery voltages.

And, if we're not satisfied with the readings at that point, we'll go ahead and load up theLMPbus and - put a load on it rather - and then check the battery performance both by itself and sharing a load on theLMPbus.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: I understand what you're going to do.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Let's press on, and I'll read the items off in checklist fashion.
And I'll read one or two items at a time, Ed, depending on if they're in the same proximity, and wait for your verification.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Up front, let's turn the Floodlights to All.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: The Descent Water and Descent Oxygen, both Open.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And on panel 16, Cabin Repress circuit breaker, closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And, Kitty Hawk, we're ready to transfer toLMpower now, and if you'll give us the mark, we'll get the time.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: The Translunar Bus Tie breakers, both closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Over on the other side on 16, Signal Conditioner 2, closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: UnderEPS, the Displays and the DescentECAcontrol, both closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Ed makes a tiny slip of the tongue here.
DECA is short for Descent Engine Control Assembly, not for the Descent Stage Electronics Control Assembly.
He is quick to correct himself.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Now verify the Descent Batteries, all four talkbacks are on Low and the Descent Battery talkback is gray.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: On panel 16, under Comm, Primary S-Band, both breakers, closed, and the PMP, closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Comm, Primary S-Band power amp Transmitter/ Receiver and PMP, closed.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And while we're doing this, you can keep your eye on that bus voltage - whether or not we need to go to high taps.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And on 16, AscentECAand AscentECAControl, both closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now, on your audio panel on theLMPside, S-Band T/R to T/R.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And under Comm, under the S-Band, PM, Prime, Prime, Off,PCM.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And then Off/Reset on your Range.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now on your Telemetry, Ed, we'll want Off and Hi.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now, on the S-Band Antennas, you might take a quick check and see which is your best Omni there.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Well, since we don't have those meters powered up, I don't have anything to tell by.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, now stand by 1; we'll see if we're ready to press on here.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: We're ready to press on, and, for your information on telemetry here, we're still showing that Ascent about 3/10ths of a volt low, ascent 5.
We're ready to pick up now on panel 11.EPSCross Tie Balance Loads, open.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: That makes them both open, now.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Now we're going to turn off Batteries 3 and 4, so Battery 3 and 4 Hi Voltage to Off/ Reset.
And you may get a DC feeder-fault light here.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Bat 3 and 4, you want the Off/Reset.
I have bos - both Balance Loads and Bus - Cross Tie Bus, open.
In other words, you want to dump the Commander's Bus.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And I have a feeder fault.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You do have a feeder fault?
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now on panel 11, Ed, they want to open all the circuit breakers on panel 11, except Signal Conditioner 1, the DC Bus Volt, and both Bat Feed Tie breakers.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: I want them all open except Signal Conditioner l, the DC Bus Volt.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Joe, I'm - Say again the ones you want closed.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We have four that we want closed.
They are the Signal Conditioner - Signal Conditioner 1, Bat Bus Volts, and both Bat Feed Tie breakers.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Give me the last three again.
I have the Signal Conditioner, but I still don't get the last three.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The DC Bus Volts and both Bat Feed Tie breakers.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Both Bat Feed Ties, DC Bus Volts, and the Signal Conditioner.
I'm now pulling the Rendezvous Radar Heater, the Standby breaker, the Landing Radar Heater breaker, Annunciating/ Docking/Component lighting breaker,IMUStandby, DescentECA, Translunar Bus Tie.
I now have four bus - four circuit breakers closed, the remainder open.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And you have theIMUStandby breaker open.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: We'd like to bring Battery 5 Back Up, On.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, Battery 5 Back Up Commander Feed is On.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And onboard there, if you'd compare both your battery and bus voltages and give us a readout, please.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, Battery 5 is reading about 35.8, and the Commander's Bus is reading 35.8.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Very good, now give us battery and bus voltages, please.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, I read 36 on the bus, 36 on the battery.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Now, Battery 5 Backup to On, and when you do it, see if Battery 6 pulls any of Battery 5 load.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And, looks like the volt on - the voltage on 6 rose just a little bit, and I see no change in current.
It appears that it's sharing the load; they're both at 36 plus.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You do have Battery 5 on Back Up and Battery 6 on Normal.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Stand by 1 now, and let us look at the stuff here.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: With your concurrence, I was looking at 6 when I brought 5 Back Up, On.
Let me shift it - my Temp Monitor - Power/Temp Monitor - and watch 5 and cycle them, if that's okay.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: First could you verify AscentECAControl breaker is closed, please.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now we'll take - we'd like you to take Battery 6 to Off momentarily and then back to Normal power, and you can watch Battery 5 voltage there on your monitor.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now when I cycle Battery 6, the Battery 5 voltage comes up when Battery 6 is brought on the line.
It drops down when Battery 6 is off the line.
However, by watching 6, I do not see any current drain.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're seeing the same thing here, Ed.
And stand by just a moment in this configuration now.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Let's take Battery 5 Back Up - correction - Battery 6 Normal Feed, Off.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: We can turn Battery 3 Hi Volt and Battery 4 Hi Volt back On now?
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, Battery 3 Hi Volt is On.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: If you have your activation - Do you have your activation checklist there with you?
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, go ahead and configure panel 11 now as per page 1-3, and I'll have four changes when you - when you get the breakers configured there.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And give me a call when you get panel 11 configured there.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now I've got four breakers I'd like for you to change on 11.
The Cross Tie Balance Load breaker, open.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Cross Tie Balance Loads is open.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I've closed the following circuit breakers, in addition to 13: Signal Conditioner 1, Glycol Pump 2, Translunar Bus Tie, and I've opened the Cross Tie Balance Loads.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: What we want to do, now, is put some loads on theLMPBus for the load test.
Now you can go over to panel 16, Ed.
And on panel 16, I'd like the - under Instrumentation - the Signal Sensor, closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And under Heaters, theRCSSystem A and B, all four heaters - circuit breakers, closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Under Heaters,RCSSystem A/B-2 Quads 1, 2, 3, 4 are closed.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Now back on panel 11 we have AC Bus A and B, Bus Tie Inverter breakers, all four of them, closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: AC Bus A and B, I've closed the four Bus Tie Inverter breakers.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Under AC Bus A, the AC Bus Volts, closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Under AC B, the System Engineer Window Heater, closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: System Engineer is the Grumman term for the Lunar Module Pilot, reflecting his role as someone who monitors the onboard equipment during LM operations.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: And under AC Bus A, the Commander's Window Heater, closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, now if you'd scurry back over to the other side under 16, we haveEPSInverter 2, closed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now if you'll check the AC Bus and turn on Inverter 2 - turn Inverter switch to number 2.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And I'm reading right at the high end of the green band.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Now, turn your Monitor to Battery 1, and we'd like theRCSSystem A/B-2 Quad Heaters, all four, to the Manual position, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now let's bring Battery 5 Normal switch On.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And Battery 5 seemed to have picked up - picked up about 12 amps indicated.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And is sharing the load with 1 and 2, apparently.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And standby just a few more seconds, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now let's start bringing - taking the descent batteries off.
First of all, we'd like Battery 1 to Off/Reset.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And let's remain in this configuration for a few seconds here.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, Ed, now bring Battery 2, Off/Reset.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Battery 2 is Off, and Bat 5 is carrying the whole load at 30 amps.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Give us about a minute here, Ed, and we'll press on to the next step.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Sure have a good - Moon view out the Commander's window here.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: This appears to be the first of series of remarks that Mitchell makes to indicate that he would rather start preparing for the rapidilly approachingLOIburn then to continue to work a missing 0.3 volt problem.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: We're ready to press on now.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: We'll remain here for a few seconds and take a look at her ...
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: 5 and 6 seem to be sharing the load.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Now, let's take Batteries 1 and 2 Hi Volt to On.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now on the circuit breakers over there, theRCSSystem A/B-2 Quads, all four breakers, open.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: You can turn all four heater switches to the Off position.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now turn the Inverter switch, Off.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now let's finish deactivating here - that wasn't - that looks like a good check.
Can - on your audio panel, you can turn your S-Band T/R, Off.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And I guess we want to stand by here just a moment, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: What we're doing right now, Ed, is just watching the open circuit voltage on thatTMhere for a few minutes to make sure it's stable.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And using up good juice as well.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Mitchell is worried that they are making a minor shortfall in battery capacity worse by burning up so much power during these checks.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Can we press on and get powerdown?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We're just about ready to press on, Ed.
We're going now with the intercomms, S-Band PM, Off, Off, Off.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: You have PM, Off, Off, Off, Off, Off/Reset.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: UnderEPS, Cross Tie Balance Loads, open.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Cross Tie Balance Loads are open.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, and you can select low taps on batteries.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now you can go back to that activation checklist and configure the - both circuit breaker panels as per page 1-3 and 1-4 and I'll stand by.
And if you will, give me a call when you're finished with that.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: If you're ready, you can transfer back toCSMpower now, and we'll get the time, if you'll give us a mark.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: I still haven't got 16 configured yet, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Here it comes back to theCSM.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: By flipping a switch on the Main Display Console, Stu has sent a signal to the LM's Electric Power System that disconnects the Descent batteries and instead stars drawing power via the umbilical connectors using power from Command Module Main Bus B.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Okay, Ed, now on yourECSpanel, you can close the Descent Water and Oxygen.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: On panel 11, on your circuit breakers theEPS, DC Bus Volt, open.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: And on panel 16 underECS, Cabin Repress, open.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: You can roll the window shades back up now, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: The window shades are going up, and the old Moon is sure getting big in the Commander's - Commander's window at this point.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You're not very far from there, on the chart up here, that's for sure.
Okay, Ed, on panel 3, Floodlight, Off.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Growing noticeably in the last hour.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And on panel 3 now, Floodlight, Off.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Okay, Ed, the Cabin Relief and Dump Overhead to open.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Now you canIVTback to the Command Module, and close the hatch behind you.
And we thank you very much.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Is everybody satisfied with the checkout, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: And, Ed, we'll have some more elaborate words for you on those batteries.
The battery 5 holds the load real good.
It stands up under the load real good.
I think they want to evaluate a few more parameters here, and so far it looks like there's nothing to worry about.
transcript.speaker: Engle

tokens: Shall we put the hardware back in the tunnel?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Roger, Al, you can go ahead, and put the hardware back in and get back into normal configuration.
transcript.speaker: Engle
